question_text: What is the normal in the context of reflection and refraction?

options.label: A
options.text: The surface of the mirror or lens

options.label: B
options.text: An imaginary line perpendicular to the surface at the point where light hits

options.label: C
options.text: The path that light travels before hitting a surface

options.label: D
options.text: The angle between the light ray and the surface

correct_answer: An imaginary line perpendicular to the surface at the point where light hits
explanation: The normal is an imaginary line drawn at 90 degrees (perpendicular) to a surface. It acts as a reference line used to measure angles of incidence and reflection in both reflection and refraction. This is a fundamental concept used when applying the laws of reflection and refraction.
